info.name: Datastore API
info.description: CKAN-compatible datastore API — auto-generated from `example_payload/`. Set `baseUrl` to your server, `apiKey` to a CKAN API key (anonymous reads are allowed; writes require a key), and `resourceId` to the table you want to hit.

item.name: health
item.description: Liveness / readiness probes live under `/datastore/api` (`/datastore/api/health`, `/datastore/api/ready`); the welcome banner is at the root `/`. Hit any of these to check the server.

request.description: Banner / root endpoint. Echoes `APP_MESSAGE`.
Liveness probe — always 200 while the process is up.
Readiness probe — 200 only when both engines pass healthcheck.

item.name: datastore_create
item.description: Declare a resource and optionally seed it with rows. Run this first. Accepts either the canonical Frictionless `schema` or the legacy `fields` + `primary_key` shape.

request.description: Canonical input: pass a Frictionless Table Schema under `schema` and (optionally) seed rows under `records`.
Back-compat path: the legacy `fields` + `primary_key` shape against a resource id that already exists in CKAN.
Back-compat path: declare a brand-new CKAN resource inline with `resource: {…}` plus legacy `fields` / `primary_key`.

item.name: datastore_upsert
item.description: Write rows. `method` picks the strategy: `upsert` (default — match by primaryKey, insert new), `insert` (fail on duplicate), or `update` (fail on miss). Run after `datastore_create`.

request.description: Match each row by stored `primaryKey`; update on hit, insert on miss. Updates only bump `_updated_at` when a non-PK column actually changes.
Insert only; duplicate primary key surfaces as a clean ValidationError.
Update only; any row whose `primaryKey` doesn't match an existing row raises NotFoundError.

item.name: datastore_info
item.description: Read the resource's column schema + row count. Useful for confirming the table exists and verifying writes landed.

request.description: Return the stored Frictionless schema + row count via `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_STORAGE`.
`id` is accepted as a legacy CKAN alias for `resource_id`.

item.name: datastore_search
item.description: Stream rows matching `filters` / `q`. Pagination via `limit` + `offset`; response carries `_links.next` / `prev` / `page_size` / `page` / `total_pages`.

request.description: Default page (limit=100, offset=0, include_total=true).
JSON-encoded filters on the URL. Value matches must respect column types (no JSON-column equality).
BigQuery `SEARCH(row, @q)` against every text column.
Custom projection (CSV), multi-column sort, explicit limit / offset / include_total. Drives `_links.next`.

item.name: datastore_search_sql
item.description: Run a vetted `SELECT` / `WITH` statement. `LIMIT` is required (parsed from the SQL); pagination links rewrite the SQL's `OFFSET` so callers can follow `next` without editing.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=SELECT auction_id, product_code, clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true LIMIT 100

query.value: SELECT auction_id, product_code, clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true LIMIT 100

request.description: Plain SELECT with WHERE + LIMIT. Total comes from `INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_STORAGE` since there's no aggregate.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=SELECT product_code, AVG(clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh) AS avg_price, SUM(volume_mwh) AS total_volume FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true GROUP BY product_code ORDER BY product_code LIMIT 50

query.value: SELECT product_code, AVG(clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh) AS avg_price, SUM(volume_mwh) AS total_volume FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true GROUP BY product_code ORDER BY product_code LIMIT 50

request.description: Aggregates collapse rows, so total goes through the `COUNT(*) FROM (<inner>)` path instead of the metadata shortcut.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=WITH daily AS (SELECT DATE(delivery_start) AS d, product_code, AVG(clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh) AS price FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true GROUP BY d, product_code) SELECT * FROM daily ORDER BY d DESC, product_code LIMIT 50

query.value: WITH daily AS (SELECT DATE(delivery_start) AS d, product_code, AVG(clearing_price_gbp_per_mwh) AS price FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" WHERE accepted = true GROUP BY d, product_code) SELECT * FROM daily ORDER BY d DESC, product_code LIMIT 50

request.description: Common table expression. CTE aliases are excluded from auth + qualification (they're inline, not external tables).

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=SELECT _id, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" ORDER BY _id ASC LIMIT 50 OFFSET 100

query.value: SELECT _id, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" ORDER BY _id ASC LIMIT 50 OFFSET 100

request.description: Pagination via OFFSET. `_links.next` will rewrite the OFFSET in the SQL string so the caller can follow without editing.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=SELECT a._id AS a_id, b._id AS b_id, a.auction_id, a.product_code FROM "balancing_auction_results_2025" a JOIN "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" b ON a.auction_id = b.auction_id WHERE a.product_code = 'DCL' ORDER BY a.auction_id ASC LIMIT 50

query.value: SELECT a._id AS a_id, b._id AS b_id, a.auction_id, a.product_code FROM "balancing_auction_results_2025" a JOIN "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" b ON a.auction_id = b.auction_id WHERE a.product_code = 'DCL' ORDER BY a.auction_id ASC LIMIT 50

request.description: JOIN across two resource_ids. Each table is authorised independently via CKAN; both get the `project.dataset` prefix.

url.raw: {{baseUrl}}/api/3/action/datastore_search_sql?sql=SELECT '2025' AS source, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" UNION ALL SELECT '2024' AS source, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "balancing_auction_results_2024" ORDER BY delivery_start DESC LIMIT 100

query.value: SELECT '2025' AS source, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "7a10def4-8e95-46f9-96c7-9f61bdfd1a09" UNION ALL SELECT '2024' AS source, auction_id, product_code, delivery_start FROM "balancing_auction_results_2024" ORDER BY delivery_start DESC LIMIT 100

request.description: UNION ALL across two resource_ids — handy for combined reports over time-partitioned tables.

item.name: datastore_delete
item.description: Cleanup. Drop the table (no filters/fields), delete rows (`filters`), or drop columns (`fields`). `filters` and `fields` are mutually exclusive.

request.description: No `filters`, no `fields` → DROP TABLE + delete metadata row. Resource disappears entirely.
Parameterised `DELETE FROM … WHERE …` against the filter columns. JSON-column equality rejected at the boundary.
`ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN …` + rewrite the stored schema. System columns (`_id`, `_updated_at`) and PK columns are protected.
`force=true` bypasses the CKAN read-only guard.
